title: Concurrency, Idempotency, Partial Failure, and Resilient Integration · Claim Repair Board
summary: A preserved decision record for concurrency, idempotency, partial failure, and resilient integration contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in fault-aware integration and recovery harness and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled.
Define idempotency scope, result replay, conflict behavior, retention, and atomic relationship to the protected effect.
Distinguish rejected, failed, timed out, cancelled, partially completed, and unknown remote outcomes before retry or compensation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Effect Identity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A timed-out request succeeds remotely, its retry duplicates the effect, a delayed event arrives out of order, and automated recovery compounds the conflict. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled.

Inspect the Effect Identity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Effect Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Effect Identity, keep Effect Identity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Effect Identity, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Effect Identity, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Effect Identity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Effect Identity, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A timed-out request succeeds remotely, its retry duplicates the effect, a delayed event arrives out of order, and automated recovery compounds the conflict. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled.
For Inspect the failure record · Debug Effect Identity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A timestamp is a sufficient idempotency key.

stimulus.title: Effect Identity case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A timed-out request succeeds remotely, its retry duplicates the effect, a delayed event arrives out of order, and automated recovery compounds the conflict. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A timestamp is a sufficient idempotency key.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign stable operation, request, event, entity, and attempt identities so repeated delivery can be recognized and reconciled. Artifact under review: fault-aware integration and recovery harness.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Effect Identity, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Effect Identity record says fault-aware integration and recovery harness looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Effect Identity.

For inspect the failure record, apply Effect Identity without its stated preconditions, omit the fault-aware integration and recovery harness trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Effect Identity for fault-aware integration and recovery harness.
